records.stage_evidence: Clinical report with findings and impression

ocr.verbatim:
I.D. NO - 08
March 18, 2026
PATIENT NAME: 
REF. BY: DR 
M.S.
USG: ABDOMEN AND PELVIS
Liver is enlarged in size (144mm). Liver parenchyma shows homogenous echotexture. No focal lesion is seen. Intrahepatic biliary radicles are not dilated.
Gall bladder shows normal physiological distension. Wall thickness is 1.5mm. A layer of multiple very small size bright echogenic shadows are seen in the lumen - sludge particles with microliths. Posterior acoustic shadowing is seen.
CBD is 4mm in caliber.
Portal Vein is normal.
Pancreas is normal in thickness. Echotexture is normal. Clearly defined margins are seen. Pancreatic duct is not dilated.
Spleen is normal in size with homogenous echotexture.
Right kidney - is normal in size. Renal parenchymal echotexture is normal. Cortico-medullary definition is maintained. No calculus is seen. Pelviccalyceal system is not dilated. Perinephric space is normal. Right Ureter - is not dilated.
Left kidney - is normal in size. Renal parenchymal echotexture is normal. Cortico-medullary definition is maintained. No calculus is seen. Pelviccalyceal system is not dilated. Perinephric space is normal. Left Ureter - is not dilated.
Lymph nodes - are not enlarged.
Urinary bladder shows normal wall thickness. No calculus or mass is seen in the cavity.
Uterus is not visualized (H/o hysterectomy).
Others - There is no evidence of ascitis.
IMPRESSION
MICROLITHIASIS GB WITH SMALL SLUDGE PARTICLES. HEPATOMEGALY.
POST HYSTERECTOMY STATUS.
NORMAL SPLEEN, PANCREAS AND K.U.B. SCAN.
Please correlate with clinical findings.
Thanks for referral.
ULTRASOUND - COLOUR DOPPLER - SMALL PARTS STUDIES - HIGH RESOLUTION STUDIES - X-RAY
THIS IS PROFESSIONAL OPINION NOT A FINAL DIAGNOSIS. IT NEEDS CLINICAL & LABORATORY CORRELATION. REPORT IS NOT VALID FOR MEDICO - LEGAL PURPOSE.
Timing : Sunday to Friday 9am. to 7pm.  *  SATURDAY CLOSED

records.summary: This is a typed ultrasound report of the abdomen and pelvis, dated March 18, 2026, for a 47-year-old female patient. It details findings including hepatomegaly, microlithiasis with sludge in the gallbladder, and normal findings for the pancreas, spleen, kidneys, ureters, and urinary bladder. The report notes the patient has a history of hysterectomy and states the uterus is not visualized. The impression includes microlithiasis, hepatomegaly, post-hysterectomy status, and normal spleen, pancreas, and KUB scan.
key_observations: Findings include hepatomegaly (liver 144mm) and microlithiasis with sludge in gallbladder
Normal pancreas, spleen, kidneys, ureters, and urinary bladder
Report is not valid for medico-legal purposes

records.summary: Six ultrasound images of the abdomen showing the right kidney, liver, common bile duct, pancreas, gallbladder, and spleen. The gallbladder is imaged in multiple views with arrows indicating a possible pathology.
key_observations: Right kidney and liver are visualized with no obvious abnormalities.
Pancreas and common bile duct are shown in cross-section.
Gallbladder (GB) is imaged in multiple views with arrows pointing to a structure within it.
Spleen is visualized with a measurement of 144.39 mm.
Images are from a Samsung ultrasound machine with acquisition dates of 18-03-2026 and 18-05-2026.
